Cardiopulmonary Bypass (CPB) Response Analysis in Tetralogy of Fallot (ToF) and Atrial Septal Defect (ASD)

Sara Tahir

2026-04-02

Project Overview

This pipeline analyzes the transcriptomic response to cardiopulmonary bypass (CPB) in pediatric patients with Tetralogy of Fallot (ToF) and Atrial Septal Defect (ASD) stratified by sex.

Platform: Affymetrix Human Genome U133 Plus PM Array (GSE132176)

Design: Paired samples (Pre-CPB and Post-CPB) from the same individuals.

Setup and Read data

# Load/Install Manager
if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(
  tidyverse, gplots, here,      # CRAN
  GEOquery, oligo, limma, edgeR, hthgu133pluspm.db # Bioconductor
)
# Download geo dataset
# Force a more robust download method
options(download.file.method.GEOquery = "libcurl")
Sys.setenv("VROOM_CONNECTION_SIZE" = 131072 * 10) # Gives the connection more memory
geo_data <- getGEO("GSE132176", GSEMatrix = TRUE)
metadata <- pData(geo_data[[1]])

# Read files
cel_files <- list.celfiles(path = "GSE132176_RAW", full.names = TRUE)
file_gsms <- stringr::str_extract(cel_files, "GSM[0-9]+")
ordered_files <- cel_files[match(metadata$geo_accession, file_gsms)]
raw_data <- read.celfiles(ordered_files)
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852123_H09_POST-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852124_H09_PRE-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852125_H111_POST-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852126_H111_PRE-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852127_H115_POST-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852128_H115_PRE-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852129_H119_POST-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852130_H119_PRE-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852131_H139_POST-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852132_H139_PRE-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852133_H175_POST-CEC_ASD.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852134_H175_PRE-CEC_ASD.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852135_H177_POST-CEC_ASD.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852136_H177_PRE-CEC_ASD.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852137_H204_POST-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852138_H204_PRE-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852139_H227_POST-CEC_ASD.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852140_H227_PRE-CEC_ASD.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852141_H255_POST-CEC_ASD.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852142_H255_PRE-CEC_ASD.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852143_H27_POST-CEC_ASD.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852144_H27_PRE-CEC_ASD.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852145_H313_POST-CEC_ASD.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852146_H313_PRE-CEC_ASD.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852147_H315_POST-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852148_H315_PRE-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852149_H34_POST-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852150_H34_PRE-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852151_H344_POST-CEC_ASD.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852152_H344_PRE-CEC_ASD.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852153_H361_POST-CEC_ASD.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852154_H361_PRE-CEC_ASD.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852155_H62_POST-CEC_ASD.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852156_H62_PRE-CEC_ASD.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852157_H71_POST-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852158_H71_PRE-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852159_H76_POST-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852160_H76_PRE-CEC_TOF.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852161_H96_POST-CEC_ASD.cel
## Reading in : GSE132176_RAW/GSM3852162_H96_PRE-CEC_ASD.cel

Pre-Processing and Normalization

Use the Robust Multi-array Average (RMA) algorithm to perform background subtraction, quantile normalization, and log2 summarization. (Note: Unable to filter based on PA calls (Presence/Absence) because “this is a PM-only array, so MAS5-style P/A calls are not applicable.”

# Pre-RMA Distribution Check
boxplot(raw_data, target = "core", main = "Pre-Normalization: Raw Intensities", las = 2, cex.axis = 0.5)

# Use RMA to do background subtraction, normalization, and summarization
eset <- rma(raw_data)
## Background correcting
## Normalizing
## Calculating Expression
# Get ExpressionFeatureSet
expression_matrix <- exprs(eset)
# Post-RMA Distribution Check
boxplot(expression_matrix, main = "Post-RMA Normalization: Log2 Intensities", las = 2, cex.axis = 0.5)

Intensity Filtering

To increase power, drop probes that are mostly just background noise. A density plot of the probe means shows the noise floor is around an instensity of log(10), 3.31

# Average expression for every probe across all samples
probe_means <- rowMeans(expression_matrix)
# Density distribution of averages
plot(density(probe_means), 
     main = "Density of Probe Average Intensities", 
     xlab = "Log2 Intensity (RMA Normalized)")
# Red line at the log2(10) threshold (3.32)
abline(v = log2(10), col = "red", lwd = 2, lty = 2)

keep_intensity <- rowMeans(expression_matrix) > log2(10)
expression_matrix_filtered <- expression_matrix[keep_intensity, ]

Annotation of Genes

# Label genes
gene_symbols <- mapIds(hthgu133pluspm.db,
                      keys = rownames(expression_matrix_filtered),
                      column = "SYMBOL",
                      keytype = "PROBEID",
                      multiVals = "first")
## 'select()' returned 1:many mapping between keys and columns
# Filter out probes that don't map to any known gene (NAs)
valid_probes <- !is.na(gene_symbols)
expression_matrix_filtered <- expression_matrix_filtered[valid_probes, ]
hgnc_symbols_filtered <- gene_symbols[valid_probes]

Transcriptomic Sex Identification

Metadata in this dataset lacks male/female designation, so to distinguish between males and females the expression of the following genes was analyzed.

Female only: XIST

Male only: KDM5D, RPS4Y1, NLGN4Y, EIF1AY

# Create a temporary collapsed matrix just for the sex calculation math
temp_annotated <- avereps(expression_matrix_filtered, ID = hgnc_symbols_filtered)

sex_genes <- c("XIST", "KDM5D", "RPS4Y1", "EIF1AY", "NLGN4Y") 
available_sex_genes <- sex_genes[sex_genes %in% rownames(temp_annotated)]

# Subset matrix to contain just sex genes and transpose so that samples are rows
sex_matrix <- t(temp_annotated[available_sex_genes, ])

# Heiarchical clustering
dist_sex <- dist(sex_matrix)
hclust_sex <- hclust(dist_sex)

plot(hclust_sex, main="Dendrogram: Sex Determination", xlab="Samples", cex=0.5)

heatmap.2(t(sex_matrix), 
          trace = "none", scale = "row",
          col = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(50),
          main = "Sex Gene Expression Profile")

clusters <- cutree(hclust_sex, k = 2)

# Calculate the mean XIST expression for both clusters
xist_c1 <- mean(sex_matrix[clusters == 1, "XIST"])
xist_c2 <- mean(sex_matrix[clusters == 2, "XIST"])

# The cluster with the higher XIST expression is Female
female_cluster <- ifelse(xist_c1 > xist_c2, 1, 2)

# Build the final sex dataframe for the pipeline
sexDf <- data.frame(
  sampleID = rownames(sex_matrix),
  Cluster = clusters
) %>%
  mutate(
    # Clean up the sample IDs to match metadata
    sampleID = gsub("^(GSM[0-9]+)_.*$", "\\1", basename(sampleID)),
    # Assign the text labels
    transcriptomicSex = ifelse(Cluster == female_cluster, "Female", "Male")
  )

Metadata formatting

metadata$sampleID <- rownames(metadata)

# Merge sex and create clean factors
metadata <- metadata %>%
  left_join(sexDf %>% dplyr::select(sampleID, transcriptomicSex), by = c("geo_accession" = "sampleID")) %>%
  mutate(
    Time = factor(ifelse(grepl("before", `time:ch1`), "Pre", "Post"), levels = c("Pre", "Post")),
    Disease = factor(`disease state:ch1`),
    PatientID = factor(gsub("patient ", "Pt", `individual:ch1`)), 
    Sex = factor(transcriptomicSex)
  )

# Apply clean names (e.g., "TOF_Pre_Male_Pt1")
clean_sample_names <- paste(metadata$Disease, metadata$Time, metadata$Sex, metadata$PatientID, sep = "_")
colnames(expression_matrix_filtered) <- clean_sample_names
rownames(metadata) <- clean_sample_names

Sample Quality Control

Used PCA plots and hierarchical clustering. (Note: Unable to unable to create NUSE/RLE plots because R crashes upon calling fitProbeLevelModel)“U

# Hierarchical clustering
dist_mat <- dist(t(expression_matrix_filtered))
hclust_res <- hclust(dist_mat)
plot(hclust_res, 
     main = "Hierarchical Clustering of Samples", 
     xlab = "Samples", 
     sub = "", 
     cex = 0.6) # cex shrinks the text so patient IDs don't overlap as badly

# Heatmap of top variable genes
# Calculate variance for all genes across all samples
gene_vars <- apply(expression_matrix_filtered, 1, var)
# Grab top 500 genes
top_var_genes <- names(sort(gene_vars, decreasing = TRUE))[1:500]

heatmap.2(expression_matrix_filtered[top_var_genes, ], 
          trace = "none",         
          scale = "row",          # Scales the colors per gene (row) so extremes don't wash out the plot
          col = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(50),
          main = "Top 500 Variable Genes",
          cexCol = 0.6,           
          margins = c(10, 5)) 

# PCA Plot
pca_res <- prcomp(t(expression_matrix_filtered), scale. = TRUE)
pca_df <- as.data.frame(pca_res$x)
pca_plot_data <- cbind(pca_df, metadata)

# Create a combined grouping variable for the colors
pca_plot_data$Disease_Sex <- paste(pca_plot_data$Disease, pca_plot_data$Sex, sep = "_")

ggplot(pca_plot_data, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Disease_Sex, shape = Time)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_bw() +
  
  # Assign complementary light/dark shades so the diseases group visually
  scale_color_manual(
    name = "Disease & Sex",
    values = c(
      "ASD_Female" = "orange", 
      "ASD_Male" = "firebrick", 
      "TOF_Female" = "deepskyblue", 
      "TOF_Male" = "darkblue"
    )
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "PCA of Filtered Expression Data",
    subtitle = "Single Panel: Disease/Sex (Color) and Time (Shape)",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", round(summary(pca_res)$importance[2,1] * 100, 1), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", round(summary(pca_res)$importance[2,2] * 100, 1), "%)")
  )

Batch Effect Assessment

Physical scan dates were extracted to assess temporal batch effects.

raw_dates <- protocolData(raw_data)$dates

metadata$ScanDate <- as.factor(as.Date(raw_dates, format = "%Y/%m/%d"))
metadata$ScanYear <- as.factor(format(as.Date(raw_dates, format = "%Y/%m/%d"), "%Y"))

metadata$SampleGroup <- paste(metadata$Disease, metadata$Time, sep = "_")
table(Group = metadata$SampleGroup, Date = metadata$ScanDate)
##           Date
## Group      2013-03-05 2013-03-21 2013-03-27 2013-03-28 2013-04-11 2013-05-07
##   ASD_Post          1          0          0          0          0          0
##   ASD_Pre           1          0          1          0          2          2
##   TOF_Post          0          0          0          0          0          0
##   TOF_Pre           0          2          3          1          0          0
##           Date
## Group      2014-01-24 2014-02-26 2014-02-28 2014-05-23 2014-06-17 2014-06-26
##   ASD_Post          3          1          1          1          0          1
##   ASD_Pre           0          0          0          1          0          1
##   TOF_Post          1          3          2          2          0          2
##   TOF_Pre           0          0          1          0          3          0
##           Date
## Group      2014-07-04
##   ASD_Post          2
##   ASD_Pre           2
##   TOF_Post          0
##   TOF_Pre           0
pca_plot_data$ScanDate <- metadata$ScanDate
pca_plot_data$ScanYear <- metadata$ScanYear

ggplot(pca_plot_data, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = ScanDate, shape = Time)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA Checking for Batch Effects (Scan Date)",
    subtitle = "Shape denotes Pre (Circle) vs Post (Triangle)",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", round(summary(pca_res)$importance[2,1] * 100, 1), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", round(summary(pca_res)$importance[2,2] * 100, 1), "%)")
  )

year_colors <- c("2013" = "dodgerblue", "2014" = "firebrick")

ggplot(pca_plot_data, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = ScanYear, shape = Time)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_manual(values = year_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "PCA Checking for Batch Effects (Scan Year)",
    subtitle = "Visualizing the temporal confounding effect",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", round(summary(pca_res)$importance[2,1] * 100, 1), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", round(summary(pca_res)$importance[2,2] * 100, 1), "%)")
  )

Limma DGE

# Primary grouping factor, ex: TOF_Post_Male
group <- factor(paste(metadata$Disease, metadata$Time, metadata$Sex, sep = "_"))
# Set up design matrix
design <- model.matrix(~0 + group)
colnames(design) <- levels(group)

# duplicateCorrelation estimates the internal consistency of each child's samples
corfit <- duplicateCorrelation(temp_annotated, design, block = metadata$PatientID)

fit <- lmFit(temp_annotated, design, block = metadata$PatientID, correlation = corfit$consensus)

# Create contrasts
contrastMatrix <- makeContrasts(
  TOF_Male_Response = TOF_Post_Male - TOF_Pre_Male,
  TOF_Female_Response = TOF_Post_Female - TOF_Pre_Female,
  ASD_Male_Response = ASD_Post_Male - ASD_Pre_Male,
  ASD_Female_Response = ASD_Post_Female - ASD_Pre_Female,
  levels = design
)

# Final Statistics
fit2 <- contrasts.fit(fit, contrastMatrix)
fit2 <- eBayes(fit2, robust = TRUE)

# View Summaries
resultsSummary <- decideTests(fit2)
print(summary(resultsSummary))
##        TOF_Male_Response TOF_Female_Response ASD_Male_Response
## Down                1018                 100                 0
## NotSig             16077               17336             17634
## Up                   548                 207                 9
##        ASD_Female_Response
## Down                    70
## NotSig               17525
## Up                      48
vennDiagram(resultsSummary, cex = c(1, 0.6, 1))
